text.title: acceptance-criteria Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for acceptance-criteria as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Too vague: "User can log in" — no context, no error handling, no boundary conditions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vague criteria cannot produce test cases; every meaningful word in a criterion must be independently testable.

failure-mode.description: Implementation details: "Store session token in Redis with 30-minute TTL" — the how, not the what.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Implementation criteria couple the spec to a specific technical choice; when the implementation changes the criteria become false without the behavior changing.

failure-mode.description: Only happy path: no error scenarios, no boundary conditions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Error paths are discovered during UAT; correcting them after development costs 10x more than specifying them upfront.

failure-mode.description: Too many criteria for one story: 50+ criteria indicate the story should be split.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A story with 50 criteria takes a sprint to test; split into smaller stories that can be independently accepted and deployed.
